summary: Phenomenal soundboard recording with crystal‑clear mix and a show highlighted by So Many Roads, Help > Slip > Franklin’s, and an epic Terrapin Station.
review: The recording is a soundboard capture praised for its crispness, perfect balance of audience ambience and instrument clarity, and overall phenomenal fidelity. Musically the concert shines – So Many Roads is repeatedly called a gem, the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence is described as flawless, and Terrapin Station, Stella Blue, Althea and other late‑’90s highlights rank among the decade’s best performances.

ai_rating.rationale: All eight reviews are overwhelmingly enthusiastic about both the performance and the sound quality, with multiple 5‑star ratings and no substantive criticism.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note a crisp, clear mix; just enough audience bleed to convey venue energy; no reported dropouts or muddiness – described as phenomenal and great sounding.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as stellar throughout, second set builds into a powerhouse with tight transitions – Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s leads into a soaring Terrapin Station and a flawless post‑drum jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals on So Many Roads are called intense and emotional; guitar work is described as bittersweet, passionate and "ass‑off" with only a minor lyric slip on Liberty that he promptly fixes.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised as the heart of the performance, providing solid bass foundation throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drum section noted as tight and "right on," with a nice jam into drums; overall drum work described as locked, loaded and fully supporting the improvisations.
